key_themes: Geopolitical tensions (Iran/Middle East) driving refining margin and supply tightness tailwinds
$900M Q1 mark-to-market hedging derivatives loss causing short-term pressure and stock pullback
Strong Wall Street analyst support with repeated Buy ratings and upward price target revisions (avg ~$180, highs to $213)
Long-term growth in midstream, chemicals, renewables transition, and sustained crack spreads
Recent pullback viewed as buying opportunity after solid YTD performance
summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) stock has experienced a recent pullback to the $156–161 range amid a disclosed ~$900 million mark-to-market hedging loss tied to surging oil prices from Middle East tensions, contributing to short-term negative sentiment and volatility.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PSX/)[[2]](https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PSX) However, analysts remain overwhelmingly positive with numerous Buy ratings and price target raises (average around $180–$190, implying meaningful upside), citing expected strong Q1 EPS growth (190% YoY), resilient refining margins, and long-term diversification benefits.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PSX/)[[3]](https://www.cnbc.com/quotes/PSX) Social and investor discussion highlights $PSX as an energy name positioned to benefit from higher oil margins, supply shocks, and non-snapping supply recovery post-conflict, with the dip seen as a constructive entry after ~24% YTD gains.[[4]](https://www.tikr.com/blog/phillips-66-is-up-24-this-year-heres-where-the-stock-could-head-in-2026)
sources: 15+ news articles (Yahoo Finance, TipRanks, Seeking Alpha, Zacks, GuruFocus), 8+ relevant X posts discussing $PSX in energy/refining context, Analyst consensus from 10–20 firms (Citi, Wells Fargo, UBS, BMO, Piper Sandler, Barclays, etc.)
